seoTitle: Set Up QuickBooks Payments for Card and ACH
metaDescription: How to activate QuickBooks Payments to accept credit card and ACH bank transfer payments directly on your invoices.
intro: Enabling QuickBooks Payments lets your clients pay invoices online through a card swipe or direct ACH transfer, without routing through a separate third-party processor. This guide covers the merchant onboarding process, setting your deposit account, and turning on payment options for invoices.

steps.heading: Open Account and Settings
steps.body: Click the Gear icon and select Account and Settings, then go to the Payments tab in the left-hand sidebar.

steps.heading: Start the merchant application
steps.body: Click the Learn More or Connect button inside the QuickBooks Payments section to begin onboarding.

steps.heading: Enter your business details
steps.body: Fill in your business structure and owner information in the secure merchant form.

steps.heading: Link your deposit account
steps.body: Select the checking account where your payouts should land, then click Activate Payments to run the automated approval.

steps.heading: Enable payment options and test
steps.body: Check the boxes for Credit Card and ACH to enable both globally, then create a test invoice to confirm the payment links appear correctly.

faq.question: How long does merchant approval take?
faq.answer: The setup uses an automated instant approval system, though final confirmation can vary depending on your business details.

faq.question: Can I offer only ACH and not credit card, or vice versa?
faq.answer: Yes, the invoice defaults let you check or uncheck Credit Card and ACH independently to control which options customers see.

faq.question: Where do processed payments actually deposit?
faq.answer: They settle into the bank account you selected during setup, and you can track the payout timeline in the Deposits ledger.

faq.question: Can I see how much I'm paying in processing fees?
faq.answer: Yes, the built-in merchant fees dashboard inside the Payments panel tracks your processing costs over time.
